data.title: Cirrus Logic in spotlight as KeyBanc ups price target
data.description:
• Cirrus Logic (CRUS) was in the spotlight on Monday as KeyBanc Capital Markets upped its price target on the specialty semiconductor company.
  • Shares were little changed in premarket trading.
  • “We see mixed implications, as positive iPhone demand and build trends should support upside to near-term estimates,” analyst John Vinh wrote in a note to clients. “However, the staggered launch for the iPhone 18 is likely to result in negative y/y growth in 2H26. We’re increasing our PT as the stock has approached our prior PT. We believe LT, CRUS remains well positioned to sustain content growth at AAPL, while we believe in the SoundWire opportunity in PC NB and view this as a sustainable opportunity capable of reaching 10% of revenues.”
  • Vinh reiterated his Overweight rating on Cirrus and upped his price target to $175 from $150.

data.title: MicroCloud Hologram to invest $400M in Bitcoin protocol upgrade for quantum security
data.description:
• MicroCloud Hologram (NASDAQ: HOLO) said on Monday that it had planned to invest over $400M to develop a quantum-resistant upgrade for the Bitcoin protocol.
  • The company had aimed to build a hybrid cryptographic framework combining ECDSA with post-quantum signature algorithms, enabling gradual network transition.
  • The protocol had been designed to remain compatible with the existing Bitcoin ecosystem, with a soft-fork upgrade path to avoid disruption.
  • The initiative had included support for multiple post-quantum algorithms like CRYSTALS-Dilithium, SPHINCS+, and a modular architecture for flexibility.
  • The company had proposed performance optimizations, including signature aggregation and hardware acceleration, to address scalability concerns.
  • The rollout had been expected to follow a phased approach, from testnet validation to full mainnet implementation
  • The company had held over $390M in cash reserves, positioning it to fund its quantum security and blockchain initiatives
  • Shares -2.94%.

data.title: Nuvini to acquire 51% stake in Beyondsoft’s U.S. business
data.description:
• Nuvini Group (Nasdaq: NVNI) on Monday said that it has agreed to acquire a 51% controlling stake in the American business of Beyondsoft Corporation in a deal valued at about $80.7 million.
  • The company said the transaction implies an enterprise value of roughly $158 million and is expected to create a combined technology platform with pro forma revenue of about $148 million.
  • Nuvini said the deal, expected to close by July 2026, will be paid in two installments, with Beyondsoft retaining a 49% stake in the business.
  • NVNI +23.56% premarket to $1.94.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: TowneBank announces sale of Resort Property Management Segment for $250M
data.description:
• TowneBank (TOWN) today announced on Monday it has completed a transaction to sell its Resort Property Management segment, Towne Vacations, to Belcrest Vacations Acquisitions for a transaction value of $250M.
  • “The transaction is financially compelling and provides the business with a partner who is well positioned to support its future growth. I would like to personally thank the dedicated teams that worked hard to complete the deal,” said William T. Morrison, President and CEO of Towne Financial Services Group.
  • “As we look to the future, we will continue to seek new opportunities in the marketplace that have the potential to create intrinsic value for our shareholders,” added William I. Foster III, President and CEO.
  • Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz served as lead legal counsel, with Williams Mullen as local counsel to TowneBank in the transaction.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OSI Systems wins over $40M medical device components contract
data.description:
• OSI Systems (OSIS) on Monday said it has received a multi-year award valued at more than $40 million to supply electronic sub-assemblies for a medical device manufacturer.

  • The company said the components will be used in the customer’s medical device platforms, under the contract awarded to its optoelectronics and manufacturing division.

  • OSIS +0.46% premarket to $269.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Netflix earns bullish call at Goldman on revenue growth outlook, capital returns
data.description:
With the distraction of battling Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for control of Warner Bros. (WBD) behind it, Netflix (NFLX) can now focus on its “strategic roadmap” of capital allocation towards content, including more live events, and returning capital to shareholders.  

With a more positive risk/reward and in respect to Netflix’s (NFLX) content slate, Goldman Sachs upgrades the stock to Buy from Neutral and lifts its price target by 20% to $120, representing 26% upside to current levels.

Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan argues that Netflix (NFLX) should achieve double-digit revenue growth over the next three to four years thanks to its advertising business—expected to grow to ~$9.5B by 2030—and increased subscription prices.

Sheridan also sees Netflix (NFLX) achieving 250 basis points of annual GAAP operating income margin expansion over the next three years, supported by cost discipline, including “moderate” spending on content.

Finally, with the pursuit of Warner Bros. (WBD) over, Netflix (NFLX) can begin returning capital to shareholders with the likelihood that 20% to 25% of its market cap will go back to investors over the next five years.

Sheridan joins a majority of Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors who view Netflix (NFLX) as a Buy. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, gives the stock a Hold rating with a Quant score of 3.23 out of 5.

Netflix (NFLX) reports first-quarter results after the market close on April 16, expected to have earned a non-GAAP profit of $0.77 per share on $12.17B in revenue.

data.title: National Healthcare Properties files preliminary prospectus for IPO of class A shares
data.description:
National Healthcare Properties (HLTC) on Monday said in a preliminary prospectus filed with the SEC that it is offering class A shares in an initial public offering.

The shares are expected to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol NHP.

Net proceeds are expected to be used to repay outstanding debt under HLTC's revolving credit facility, to fund external growth, and for other general corporate purposes.

The number of shares to be sold and the price range for the proposed offering are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: CrowdStrike announces $500M increase to share repurchase program
data.description:
• CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD) on Monday announced the repurchase of up to an additional $500 million of Class A common stock to its existing stock repurchase program, bringing the total authorization under the program to $1.5 billion.
  • Under its existing share repurchase program, CrowdStrike has repurchased 413,130 shares of its outstanding Class A common stock at an average price of $364.57 per share, for an aggregate purchase price of $150.6M.
  • "We have increased our share repurchase authorization to $1.5 billion to opportunistically return value to shareholders as we progress toward our goal of reaching $20 billion in ending ARR by FY36," said CrowdStrike’s CFO Burt Podbere.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Valaris, Halliburton in pact to develop Petronas assets offshore Suriname
data.description:
Valaris (VAL) and Halliburton (HAL) said Monday they entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Malaysia's oil and gas company Petronas to support the development of Suriname's offshore assets.

The collaboration combines Valaris' (VAL) offshore drilling expertise and high-specification global fleet, Petronas' project stewardship and basin knowledge, and Halliburton's (HAL) subsurface, well construction, and digital solutions.

Suriname is part of the Guyana-Suriname Basin, which continues to see strong exploration success and development activity and offers long-term opportunities for operators and service providers.

Separately, Valaris (VAL) said it was awarded a 1,064-day contract extension with Petrobras (PBR) offshore Brazil for the Valaris DS-4 drillship beginning November 2027 in direct continuation of the existing program, adding ~$447M to contract backlog.

data.title: AppLovin mobile ad dominance is 'real,' but risks evolve: Wedbush
data.description:
AppLovin's (APP) presence in mobile advertising is still exceptionally strong, based on the prowess of its MAX, Adjust, and AXON platforms, Wedbush Securities said. However, there does appear to be rising competition, the investment firm added. 

“AppLovin commands the largest share of mobile gaming ad spend by a wide margin, and its walled garden — built on MAX, Adjust, and AXON — remains the strongest in the ecosystem,” analyst Alicia Reese wrote in a note to clients. “That said, our expert noted a few dynamics worth monitoring: Meta is reclaiming some iOS share of voice after years of post-ATT decline. While individual studio spend with AppLovin is moderating as pricing rises, AppLovin's overall footprint continues to expand as it onboards more partners, suggesting the growth engine could shift from deepening wallet share to greater breadth of relationships.”

Delving deeper, Reese said the underlying architectures of AppLovin's AXON and Unity's (U) Vector are “fundamentally similar,” but the difference comes in first-party data. “Unity's upcoming Runtime integration — which would pipe in granular, game-level telemetry including session behavior, churn points, and ad disruption data — is the most important catalyst for closing the gap,” Reese added. “Unity should begin ingesting some of this data into models this year.”

Additionally, Unity is attempting to turn itself around with new management, and it appears to be making some headway on that front, Reese added.

“The decision to deprecate ironSource signaled internal confidence rather than desperation,” Reese wrote. “The organizational shift to a less-is-more philosophy is freeing resources for the core ad network. Our expert's view was that if Unity's one-stop-shop vision (engine + monetization + Runtime data) comes together, the company has 'as big an opportunity as anyone.'”

Reese also pointed to Meta Platforms (META) as a competitor in gaming, as its iOS share of voice in gaming has climbed “meaningfully” over the past six months. 

“Both AppLovin and Unity are aware of this shift, though neither appears to be aggressively defending against it yet, as market-share loss has likely been minimal and has not materially impeded growth for either,” Reese added. “Meta doesn't currently offer a mediation platform, but our expert noted significant publisher appetite to test one if launched.”
